reforms.description: The Swiss government proposed an employment programme targeted at the construction sector, which includes an 'investment bonus' to subsidize infrastructure investments by municipalities and cantons.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment bonus is designed to stimulate infrastructure investments, which can enhance economic activity and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been put into action as indicated by its implementation status.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the reform is significant in its support for infrastructure investment, it does not represent a structural overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: A proposal for an employment programme targeted at the construction sector has been submitted for parliamentary approval.

reforms.description: The Swiss federal authorities decided to exempt highly skilled specialists from the provisions stipulating priority for indigenous workers, allowing for greater flexibility in hiring foreign talent in the research sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform aims to enhance the talent pool in the research sector, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The decision has been enacted, allowing for the hiring of foreign specialists.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant in improving labor market flexibility for skilled workers but does not constitute a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: To meet the economy's demand for highly skilled specialists and ensure job creation and maintenance in the research sector, the federal authorities decided to exempt this category of personnel, as of 1 May, from the provisions stipulating priority for indigenous workers.
